opener.dateline:
Legation of the United States,
Guatemala and Honduras,
Guatemala, October 11, 1894. (Received October 25.)

div.p: Sir: I have the honor to herewith inclose a note from the minister of foreign relations of Guatemala, and a translation of the same, in reply to my note to him in reference to your No. 128, in relation to the request of the Chinese minister in Washington, that the United States minister at Guatemala be directed to use his good offices in behalf of the Chinese residents in this Republic when they might be required.

opener.dateline:
Department of Foreign Relations of the Republic of Guatemala, Central America,
National Palace, Guatemala, October 3, 1894.

frus:attachment.p: I have had the pleasure to receive your excellency’s polite note of the 24th ultimo, informing me that the minister of China at Washington has requested the U. S. Government to allow its diplomatic representative in Guatemala to use his good offices in behalf of Chinese residents here, with which request your excellency’s Government is disposed to comply, provided that such a step meets the approval of the Government of Guatemala.

In reply I have the honor to inform your excellency, having been authorized to do so by the President of the Republic, that my Government will have no objection to giving its approval in this case, provided the Chinese Government addresses to it the request to that end, which seems natural.
